# The Impact Of Streaming Platforms On Film Quality And Distribution

![The Impact Of Streaming Platforms On Film Quality And Distribution](https://markmurphydirector.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/The-Impact-Of-Streaming-Platforms-On-Film-Quality-And-Distribution.jpg)

As a seasoned movie director and avid cinema enthusiast, I’ve witnessed firsthand the seismic shift that streaming platforms have brought to our industry. Today, let’s dive into how these digital giants are reshaping film quality and distribution.

## The Democratisation of Content

Streaming platforms have opened doors for filmmakers who might have struggled to find traditional distribution channels. [This democratisation](https://www.allego.com/blog/the-people-have-the-power-a-case-for-the-democratization-of-content/) has led to a surge in diverse voices and narratives, increased opportunities for niche genres, and lowered barriers to entry for emerging filmmakers. The landscape of cinema has become more inclusive and varied as a result.

## Quality: A Double-Edged Sword

The impact on film quality is complex. On one hand, streaming platforms offer more creative freedom and encourage risk-taking in storytelling. Directors and writers can explore unconventional narratives that might not have found a place in traditional cinema. On the other hand, the pressure for binge-worthy content sometimes [sacrifices depth and nuance](https://spartanscoop.org/2022/12/the-decline-of-good-films/#:~:text=The%20downward%20spiral%20of%20movie,linear%20narrative%E2%80%9D%20(MOVIEWEB).) in favour of cliffhangers and plot twists designed to keep viewers clicking “next episode.”

## The New Economics of Filmmaking

Streaming has revolutionised how films are financed and distributed. [Direct-to-streaming](https://www.thecourieronline.co.uk/cinemas-to-streaming-how-is-the-rapid-transition-harming-the-industry/) releases now bypass traditional theatrical windows, changing how success is measured and profits are generated. The shift from box office returns to subscription models has influenced production decisions, sometimes favouring quantity over quality to keep subscribers engaged.

## Global Reach and Cultural Exchange

Platforms like Netflix and Amazon Prime have facilitated [easier access to international cinema](https://motionpicture.edu/socialcinema/2023/06/19/the-impact-of-streaming-services-on-the-movie-industry/), allowing audiences to explore films from around the world with just a few clicks. This has led to a fascinating cross-pollination of film styles and techniques, enriching the global cinematic landscape and exposing viewers to diverse storytelling traditions.

## The Future of Cinema

As we look ahead, key questions emerge about the future of cinema. Will theatres and streaming find a harmonious coexistence, each offering unique viewing experiences? How will AI and data analytics shape content creation, potentially influencing creative decisions based on viewer preferences and engagement metrics?

## Conclusion

Streaming platforms have undeniably transformed the landscape of filmmaking. As directors, we must adapt to these changes while preserving the essence of cinematic storytelling. The challenge lies in harnessing the opportunities of digital distribution without compromising on the quality and artistry that makes cinema magical.

What are your thoughts on how streaming has changed the film industry? Share in the comments below!

Written by Mark Murphy Director

FULL CONTENT:
# The streaming evolution: Adapting film budgeting to new media

As the entertainment landscape continues to evolve, the advent of streaming platforms has revolutionized the film industry. Film budgeting, an integral part of any production, has also undergone significant changes in this streaming era.

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20height='100'%20width='100'%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![Dmitry Vakhtin]()
![Dmitry Vakhtin](/static/fbfed3cbc00ea2f18221a38786ae2d4d/82982/-X3lj9ntmN4.jpg)
![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20height='896.7032967032967'%20width='1600'%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20version='1.1'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![The streaming evolution: Adapting film budgeting to new media]()
![The streaming evolution: Adapting film budgeting to new media](/static/8841d6e6af6838b8084fef8bb8860bc7/b6642/The-streaming-evolution---Adapting-film-budgeting-to-new-media.png)

This article aims to demystify film budgeting in the age of streaming platforms and provide valuable insights on navigating challenges and maximizing opportunities.

## **Overview of streaming platforms and the film industry**

Streaming platforms like *Netflix*, *Amazon Prime*, *Disney+*, and *Hulu* have significantly reshaped the film industry. Traditional theatrical distribution has given way to direct-to-consumer streaming services, altering revenue models and changing how filmmakers plan and budget their productions. An example is *"The Irishman" (2019)*, a *Martin Scorsese* film produced by Netflix, whose colossal budget was primarily financed by the streaming giant.

![The Irishman.jpg](https://blog.filmustage.com/content/images/2023/05/The-Irishman.jpg)

## **The impact of streaming platforms on film budgeting**

Streaming platforms have brought profound changes to film budgeting. Traditionally, budgets were drafted with considerations for theatrical release, physical distribution, and potential box office earnings. Now, filmmakers must consider digital distribution strategies, subscription-based revenue models, and the global reach of streaming platforms. The production budget for *"House of Cards"*, one of the first major *Netflix Originals*, had to account for these factors.

![House of Cards.jpg](https://blog.filmustage.com/content/images/2023/05/House-of-Cards.jpg)

## **Challenges and opportunities in film budgeting for streaming platforms**

Film budgeting for streaming platforms presents unique challenges and opportunities. Challenges include predicting audience reach, considering the global nature of streaming services, and understanding different revenue models. Opportunities lie in the potential for wider distribution, reduced physical production costs, and long-term earning potential via subscriptions. Netflix's gamble on *"The Crown"*, with its extravagant budget, reflects these challenges and opportunities.

![Crown.jpg](https://blog.filmustage.com/content/images/2023/05/Crown.jpg)

## **Strategies for effective film budgeting in the streaming age**

Adopting specific strategies can help optimize film budgeting for streaming platforms. Firstly, understanding the target audience and the platform's user base can inform budget decisions. Secondly, cost management becomes crucial, considering the long-term subscription-based revenue models. Thirdly, filmmakers should consider potential partnerships or co-productions to distribute costs, as seen in the joint venture between Netflix and BBC for *"Dracula" (2020)*.

![Dracula.jpg](https://blog.filmustage.com/content/images/2023/05/Dracula.jpg)

## **Case studies: Successful film budgeting for streaming platforms**

Several films and series illustrate successful budgeting for streaming platforms. *"Stranger Things"*, a Netflix original, demonstrated strategic budgeting by delivering high production value while maintaining cost-efficiency.

![Stranger Things.jpg](https://blog.filmustage.com/content/images/2023/05/Stranger-Things.jpg)

Similarly, *"The Mandalorian"* on Disney+ leveraged Disney's resources and integrated synergies from its Star Wars franchise to optimize its budget.

![Mandalorian.jpg](https://blog.filmustage.com/content/images/2023/05/Mandalorian.jpg)

Here are some examples of film budgets for the movies produced by streaming platforms:

Please note that these numbers are reported estimates, as exact budget details are often kept confidential by the production companies and streaming platforms.

## **Conclusion**

Film budgeting in the age of streaming platforms is a complex but rewarding process. As filmmakers continue to navigate this landscape, understanding the nuances of streaming platforms will be crucial. Challenges will persist, but the opportunities are vast. By adapting strategies and staying flexible, filmmakers can successfully plan and execute budgets that support their creative vision and ensure financial viability. In the end, the rise of streaming platforms could be seen as a new act in the ongoing drama of film budgeting, one that is replete with possibilities and potential for the global film industry.

As we continue to navigate this brave new world of streaming platforms, tools like [Filmustage](https://app.filmustage.com/?ref=blog.filmustage.com) can offer invaluable assistance. Filmustage's dynamic features are designed to support the modern filmmaker in the age of streaming, assisting in the budgeting, script breakdown, and overall film production management. By simplifying these complex processes, it can contribute to cost-effective strategies and better financial planning, making it an essential companion for filmmakers working in today's rapidly evolving industry. As we look towards the future of film, it's exciting to envision how technology like Filmustage will continue to shape our approach to film budgeting and production.

# **Changes and Challenges Coexist：how Streaming Platforms Reshape the Film Industry**

6 min readMar 20, 2025

In recent years, the digital revolution has shifted the power dynamic from traditional cinema to online streaming services, where films are readily available at the click of a button. This transformation was accelerated by the COVID-19, which forced cinemas to close, and audiences turned to platforms like Netflix, Amazon Prime and Disney+. Since then streaming media takes on the role of cinemas and becomes a “bridge” connecting the films and the audiences.

A report from the [Motion Picture Association (MPA)](https://www.motionpictures.org/) revealed that global streaming revenues surpassed box office earnings in 2020. [The global total of online video subscriptions (1.1 billion), such as Netflix and Disney+, increased by 26 percent, or 232.1 million, when comparing 2020 to 2019.](https://www.motionpictures.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/03/MPA-2020-THEME-Report.pdf) Meanwhile, [box office revenue dropped by 72% in 2020](https://www.motionpictures.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/03/MPA-2020-THEME-Report.pdf) due to theater closures. [The decrease in the global theatrical market was partially offset by a 31 percent increase in the digital home/mobile entertainment market, compared 2019.](https://www.motionpictures.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/03/MPA-2020-THEME-Report.pdf) In the post-pandemic era, the rise of streaming platforms has significantly transformed the film industry, fundamentally altering the way films are produced, distributed, and consumed.

**Changes in Film Distribution，Audiences Consume Habits and Industry Economy**

The traditional model of film distribution, where films premiered in cinemas before being released on DVDs or cable television, has undergone a great change. Streaming platforms have become the primary channel for film release, with many films now debuting directly on these platforms, bypassing traditional cinema screenings altogether. As cinemas struggled during the COVID-19, streaming services took the lead in releasing high-profile films, such as Disney’s *Mulan* and Warner Bros.’s *Wonder Woman 1984*, directly to consumers. This shift has resulted in a more flexible and accessible method of film distribution, allowing audiences to watch films on demand, anywhere, at any time.

The advent of streaming platforms also has led to a significant change in how audiences consume films. Gone are the days of waiting for a film’s television premiere or buying a physical copy. Instead, streaming platforms provide instant access to a large number of films from various genres and countries, offering an unprecedented level of convenience. The ability to watch content on multiple devices — whether it’s a TV, smartphone, tablet, or laptop — has given audiences the freedom to enjoy films in any environment, further altering the dynamics of traditional movie-going. In addition, [the technology behind streaming platforms and their recommendation algorithm allows them to cater to the certain preferences a user may want.](/digital-society/netflix-addressing-the-challenges-and-opportunities-04ac4bd5cae8)

As streaming platforms continue to grow, they are reshaping the economic model of the film industry. Traditionally, film production were expensive endeavors, relying heavily on box-office revenue and DVD sales to recoup costs. However, streaming services operate on subscription-based models, where users pay a monthly fee for access to a vast library of content. This has shifted the focus from making blockbuster hits that rely on theatrical success to producing consistent, high-quality content that appeals to broad audiences. What’s more, streaming platforms often acquire films from independent filmmakers, giving them a chance to reach global audiences that might not have been possible through traditional channels.

## Get Sabrina’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

**Challenges of the Rise of Streaming Platforms**

The rise of streaming media has significantly transformed the film industry, however it also contributes to the closure of traditional theaters and the prevalence of pirated resources. On the one hand, the ease of access, affordability, and convenience of streaming platforms have made it easier for consumers to enjoy films at home. With this shift, many people no longer feel the need to go to a cinema, leading to reduced attendance and, eventually, the closure of many traditional cinemas, which are unable to compete with the cost and convenience of home viewing.

On the other hand, streaming services have made it easier for pirated content to circulate, as unauthorized copies can be shared and streamed with little effort. Criminals record films on streaming platforms and sell them at low prices, which not only undermines the subscription model of streaming platforms, but also the copyright of filmmakers. [In 2020, there were 137.2 billion visits globally to film and TV piracy sites alone. The damage is profound, costing the US economy at least 230,000 jobs and more than $29.2 billion annually, according to a US Chamber of Commerce/NERA Economic Consulting study.](https://www.motionpictures.org/press/working-toward-a-safer-stronger-internet/)

These two problems — declining theater attendance and the proliferation of pirated content — are interlinked and could have far-reaching consequences. Movie studios and independent filmmakers may face increased financial strain, as reduced theater attendance means a loss in box office revenue. Piracy further erodes profits by allowing illegal distribution to bypass proper licensing and copyright laws. As a result, the film industry could be forced to adapt by altering distribution models, possibly leading to fewer high-budget movies or more stringent control over content sharing, limiting the creative freedom that was once a hallmark of cinema.

Governments and regulatory bodies are responding to the rise of streaming platforms by introducing new regulations that aim to protect both consumers and the industry. Traditional theaters should offer unique experiences such as special screenings, advanced technology such as IMAX, or exclusive content that cannot be easily replicated at home. Streaming services, on their part, could implement stronger digital rights management to prevent piracy and work with law enforcement to crack down on illegal platforms. Meanwhile, education on the importance of supporting legal channels and offering affordable alternatives to pirated content could help shift consumer behavior and reduce piracy’s impact.

**The Future of Streaming Platforms**

The rise of streaming platforms has reshaped the film industry, ushering in a new era of film consumption, production, and distribution. The future of film will involve a combination of traditional and digital experiences, with streaming platforms continuing to play a major role in shaping the way films are made, viewed, and enjoyed worldwide. The traditional theater experience may be challenged by the convenience and accessibility of streaming services, but there are still many opportunities for innovation and growth within the industry. Also, it will be important to address challenges such as theater attendance, and data privacy as the digital revolution continues to evolve. Balancing the growth of digital platforms with the preservation of traditional cinematic experiences will be key to the future of the film industry.

FULL CONTENT:
# [Deadbeat Films](/)

![Deadbeat Films](//images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/59d2a0a432601e71947c5acb/1507647647612-K7Q2J457JYUEKT04FYJH/DEADBEAT-FONT.png?format=1500w)

# [The Growing Impact of Streaming Platforms on Independent Filmmaking](/blog/2024/11/22/the-growing-impact-of-streaming-platforms-on-independent-filmmaking)

In recent years, streaming platforms have revolutionized the way audiences consume films, creating both opportunities and challenges for filmmakers. For independent production companies, such as those in the heart of London, this shift has been especially significant. As the streaming wars continue to evolve, independent studios are rethinking their strategies to stay relevant, competitive, and true to their creative vision.

In this blog post, we explore the ways streaming platforms are reshaping independent filmmaking, their impact on the creative process, and how companies like Deadbeat Films can navigate this new digital landscape.

## **The Streaming Boom: A Paradigm Shift for Filmmaking**

The explosion of platforms like Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, Disney+, and Apple TV+ has transformed the film industry. These platforms have not only changed how films are distributed but also redefined audience expectations.

### **Expanding Access and Opportunities**

For independent filmmakers, streaming platforms offer unprecedented opportunities to showcase their work to a global audience. Previously, the road to success for smaller productions was paved with limited theatrical releases or festival circuits. Now, a film can reach millions of viewers worldwide with the click of a button.

For production companies like Deadbeat Films, this access is invaluable. A streaming deal can catapult a low-budget project into the limelight, connecting niche stories with their ideal audiences. Moreover, platforms actively seeking unique voices have become vital partners for indie creators looking to stand out.

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/59d2a0a432601e71947c5acb/859800a8-6cae-40ae-91d7-20072ed11fbb/Mercado+Livre+-+Movie+Classifieds.png)

**Challenges of the Streaming Era for Indie Films**

While the benefits of streaming are undeniable, the rise of these platforms comes with its own set of challenges for independent filmmakers.

### **Overcrowded Marketplace**

The sheer volume of content being produced for streaming platforms has created a highly competitive marketplace. Standing out in a sea of new releases requires innovative marketing and storytelling strategies. For companies like Deadbeat Films, this means balancing creative risks with market-savvy decisions to ensure their films capture audience attention.

### **The Algorithm’s Grip on Creativity**

One downside of streaming platforms is their reliance on data-driven algorithms to decide which projects get greenlit. These algorithms favor content that fits specific viewing trends, which can discourage originality. Independent filmmakers risk being pushed to cater to formulas rather than their artistic instincts.

Deadbeat Films, however, understands the importance of maintaining creative integrity. By embracing data insights without losing sight of bold, original storytelling, the company can strike a balance that resonates with both audiences and platforms.

**Funding Indie Films in the Streaming Age**

One of the most notable shifts in the industry has been the impact of streaming on film financing. Platforms now act as both distributors and financiers, offering independent filmmakers a unique funding model.

### **Direct Funding Deals**

Streaming platforms often fund entire productions upfront, relieving filmmakers of the financial burden of traditional independent production. For companies like Deadbeat Films, this opens the door to ambitious projects that might have previously been out of reach. However, such deals often come with creative limitations, as platforms may require editorial input to align the final product with their brand.

### **Crowdfunding and Hybrid Models**

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/59d2a0a432601e71947c5acb/e6a05b2a-2446-4800-b742-e90dc4a116b9/Three+Billboards+Outside+Ebbing%2C+Missouri+-+2017.png)

Crowdfunding remains a popular choice for indie filmmakers looking to retain creative control. In some cases, filmmakers combine crowdfunding efforts with streaming partnerships to maximize both funding and audience engagement. This hybrid approach allows companies like Deadbeat Films to retain their voice while leveraging the reach of digital platforms.

**Creative Freedom vs. Platform Constraints**

The relationship between streaming platforms and independent filmmakers is complex. While platforms provide exposure and funding, they often come with strings attached.

### **The Need for Authenticity**

In the era of streaming, audiences crave authenticity. Independent filmmakers excel in this space, delivering raw, intimate stories that mainstream Hollywood often overlooks. Deadbeat Films, based in the culturally rich city of London, has a unique advantage here—drawing from the city's diverse voices to create films that feel real and relevant.

### **Adapting Without Losing Identity**

While it’s tempting to shape projects to meet streaming trends, the long-term success of an independent production company relies on its ability to retain its unique identity. For Deadbeat Films, this means staying true to their creative ethos while embracing the opportunities streaming platforms provide.

**The Role of Festivals in the Streaming Era**

Film festivals, traditionally the gateway for independent filmmakers to showcase their work, have also evolved in response to streaming.

### **Festivals as Launchpads for Streaming Deals**

Rather than solely focusing on theatrical releases, festivals now serve as marketplaces where streaming platforms acquire films. For companies like Deadbeat Films, submitting a project to major festivals like Sundance, Tribeca, or BFI London Film Festival can be a stepping stone to securing a distribution deal.

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/59d2a0a432601e71947c5acb/372bfa80-2665-4cbd-a7ff-5aee5f889b6d/Zayn+-+Vibez.png)

### **Bridging the Gap Between Theatrical and Digital**

Festivals also offer the chance to showcase films on the big screen before they transition to streaming. This hybrid approach can amplify a film's buzz and provide a unique selling point for platforms eager to blend traditional and modern viewing experiences.

**The Future: What’s Next for Independent Filmmaking?**

As streaming platforms continue to dominate the industry, independent filmmakers must adapt to stay relevant. For production companies like Deadbeat Films, the future lies in embracing change while staying rooted in their creative principles.

### **Sustainability and Inclusivity in Filmmaking**

As the industry evolves, so does the responsibility to prioritize sustainability and inclusivity. Independent companies like Deadbeat Films can lead the charge in creating environmentally conscious productions and fostering diverse talent behind and in front of the camera.

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/59d2a0a432601e71947c5acb/d9388eab-89d4-41a7-87d5-9f7753995946/Nescafe+-+Farmers.png)

**Conclusion: Thriving in the Streaming Era**

The rise of streaming platforms represents a seismic shift in the film industry, offering both opportunities and challenges for independent filmmakers. For London-based companies like Deadbeat Films, adapting to this new landscape means embracing innovation without sacrificing creativity.

By leveraging the reach of streaming platforms, experimenting with new formats, and staying true to their artistic vision, Deadbeat Films is well-positioned to thrive in this digital-first era. As the streaming wars rage on, the company’s commitment to bold storytelling and authentic filmmaking ensures it remains a vital force in contemporary cinema.

Whether you’re an aspiring filmmaker, a cinephile, or a potential collaborator, keep an eye on Deadbeat Films as they continue to navigate and shape the ever-evolving world of independent film.

### [Jack Thompson-Roylance](/blog?author=59e0cb3a5ce35039cfba64f0)

SUMMARY:
## Streaming Platforms and Their Impact on the Film Industry For better or worse, streaming has significantly impacted the film industry in terms of how films are produced and distributed and how audiences consume them. Streaming services have become movie-making powerhouses – Netflix alone has produced over 6,000 original titles, including television series, documentaries, movies, stand-up specials, and interactive specials. Should streaming services continue investing in original content production, this could lead to an even greater diversity of voices and perspectives represented in the entertainment industry. Introducing our exclusive 2-ebook bundle, Financials in Rights Management, a treasure trove of insights designed to amplify your content's reach and profitability across the streaming landscape while

FULL CONTENT:
## Streaming Platforms and Their Impact on the Film Industry

[Back to INDUSTRY INSIGHTS](https://blog.filmtrack.com/industry-insights#insights-grid-section)

When VHS was introduced in 1977 and DVDs burst onto the scene 20 years later, consumers were presented with the first viable alternatives to a movie theater. Though seen as revolutionary then, watching movies at home and bypassing theaters continues to grow. Streaming services have been available since 2005 when YouTube burst on the scene, and its pickup continues to increase sharply, particularly in the past few years. Over the last decade, movie theater attendance has declined sharply, and the list of streaming services seemingly grows every day.  

The film industry is undergoing radical change. [In 2018, 28%](https://www.thedartmouth.com/article/2022/10/trends-streaming-services#:~:text=Over%20the%20last%20decade%2C%20interest,and%20damages%20the%20industry%20irrevocably.) of consumers preferred to watch a movie for the first time in a movie theater, compared to only 15% who favored streaming. However, COVID-19 devastated theaters due to closures and the apprehension people felt about returning to theaters after the lockdown. Case in point: In 2020, [some theaters lost billions](https://variety.com/2021/film/news/amc-theatres-4-6-billion-loss-covid-1234927642/) after closing their doors due to the pandemic. By June 2020, the percentage of adults who strongly preferred seeing a movie in a theater [fell to 14%, with 36%](https://www.statista.com/statistics/947757/theaters-streaming-watching-movies/#:~:text=According%20to%20a%20study%20held,home%20than%20visit%20a%20cinema.) choosing to stream movies instead. 

For better or worse, streaming has significantly impacted the film industry in terms of how films are produced and distributed and how audiences consume them.

## How Streaming has Impacted Production and Distribution

Streaming services facilitate the production and distribution of more diverse and niche content. In addition, because they are not as limited by the traditional Hollywood studio system, streaming services can take more chances on relatively unknown filmmakers and projects, which might have a more challenging time securing funding or distribution through conventional channels. A broader range of voices and perspectives are now represented in film and television. It has made it easier for underrepresented groups to find content that echoes their experiences.

In our post, [*The Evolution of Storytelling and its Impact on Rights Management*](/industry-insights/the-evolution-of-storytelling-and-its-impact-on-rights-management), we discuss how the pandemic accelerated changes that have been coming for years, such as the collapse of the theatrical window and moving to video-on-demand, into a relatively short period of time. Now every major player has a video-on-demand platform, whether it’s a sell-through platform like iTunes, where users can purchase something to watch, a subscription-based platform like Netflix or Amazon Prime, or a combination of both.

With more and more people choosing to view films and television shows online, the number of people going to movie theaters has dropped, leading to a decline in box office sales. Television networks are also losing audience numbers as more consumers get entertainment via streaming services. However, although streaming services have led to a drop in traditional revenue, they have also introduced new revenue sources, such as subscription fees and advertising tailored directly to viewers’ interests.

## How Streaming has Impacted the Audience

Streaming services like Netflix, Amazon Prime, and Hulu have transformed media consumption. Instead of going to a physical location to rent or purchase a movie or television show, consumers can now access a vast library of content online with just a few clicks. As a result, practices like binge-watching have become common. 

One of the drivers of this change could be price: The average cost of a movie ticket at the theater is [$10.45](https://www.the-numbers.com/market/), compared to [$6.99 for a basic monthly Netflix subscription](https://www.businessinsider.com/guides/streaming/netflix-price#:~:text=A%20Netflix%20monthly%20subscription%20costs,at%20a%20time%20for%20%2419.99.). Thus, some view the growing preference for streaming services as a positive - movies are becoming more widely available and accessible for everyone.

However, streaming has also significantly impacted viewer experience and entertainment culture. Since films and television shows can be watched online, the traditional experience of traveling to a theater, purchasing a ticket, and watching a movie on a big screen is becoming less common, lessening the sense of community that watching a film with others provides.

## How Streaming Might Impact the Future of the Film Industry

Streaming services have become movie-making powerhouses – Netflix alone has produced over [6,000 original titles](https://www.whats-on-netflix.com/news/50-of-netflixs-library-is-now-made-of-netflix-originals/), including television series, documentaries, movies, stand-up specials, and interactive specials. According to Netflix, should the company continue this trajectory, it will likely hit the following milestones in the next few years:

* [July 2023: 60%](https://www.whats-on-netflix.com/news/50-of-netflixs-library-is-now-made-of-netflix-originals/) Netflix originals vs. licensed content
* [December 2024: 75%](https://www.whats-on-netflix.com/news/50-of-netflixs-library-is-now-made-of-netflix-originals/) Netflix originals vs. licensed content
* [May 2027: 100%](https://www.whats-on-netflix.com/news/50-of-netflixs-library-is-now-made-of-netflix-originals/) Netflix originals vs. licensed content

Streaming services are now challenging traditional studios as prolific producers of films and television shows. Should streaming services continue investing in original content production, this could lead to an even greater diversity of voices and perspectives represented in the entertainment industry. As these services grow in popularity, their impact on the film industry will continue to evolve, requiring traditional distribution channels to adapt and find ways to coexist.

## The Evolution of Film & TV Rights

Entertainment rights management has become increasingly fraught with complexity in tracking various rights and royalties. Our ebook, [*Complex Film & TV Rights Management: History, Current Tracking, and 21st Century Solutions*](https://info.filmtrack.com/complex-film-and-tv-rights-management-ebook), explores this evolution and looks at what a modern rights management platform should give you and how to shop for one. Dive in and download the ebook!

#### FilmTrack's Contracts & Availabilities Manager

#### FilmTrack's Financials Manager

Enjoy the most robust financial tools in the industry, built to handle the most complex calculations. Whether it’s circular deductions, financial waterfalls, or royalties in/out, you can relax and know that everything is being split and paid accurately.

enriched_points.point_title: Need for adaptation to new models
enriched_points.expanded_content: Filmmakers must embrace streaming's direct-to-consumer approach, leveraging data-driven insights to meet viewer demands while maintaining creative integrity amid evolving distribution models.
Filmmakers should consider hybrid release strategies, utilizing both theatrical and streaming platforms to maximize audience reach and revenue while preserving the cinematic experience.

The streaming era encourages filmmakers to experiment with unconventional storytelling formats, allowing for diverse narratives that resonate with global audiences and challenge traditional cinematic norms.
